purpose: Horizontal tab strip at the top of GitManagerView showing every open repository. Each tab displays a branch icon, the repo's tab label (folder basename), the current branch as a subtle chip when active, and an X close button. A trailing '+' button opens a new repository via api.ideSelectFolder() and dispatches GIT_OPEN_REPO. Clicking a tab dispatches GIT_SWITCH_REPO; clicking the X dispatches GIT_CLOSE_REPO (stopPropagation prevents tab activation). Mirrors the WorkspaceTabs.jsx pattern from the IDE view.
gotchas: Repo id generated as String(Date.now()) — same collision risk as GitWelcome (acceptable for human pace).
Reuses api.ideSelectFolder from the IDE namespace — coupling Git to IDE; if that IPC is renamed or specialized, fix this too.
GIT_CLOSE_REPO does not prompt for confirmation — closing a tab loses any in-memory state for that repo (selected file, diffs) immediately.

Only the active tab shows the current branch chip (gitCurrentBranch is global, not per-repo) — switching tabs reloads the branch for the new repo, so the old tab's branch isn't displayed even if it had one.
ideSelectFolder may return string or {path}; helper handles both shapes — the same pattern lives in GitWelcome.
patterns: Active state determined by `repo.id === gitActiveRepoId` — repo.id is the dispatch key, not the path (paths can be moved or duplicated).
Title attribute on each tab shows the full path — useful when names truncate.
stopPropagation on the close button is essential because the parent button has its own onClick.
conventions: Inline SVG icons (GitBranchTabIcon and inline path elements for + and × in the header).
